title: rosa lutetiana subsp. dumetorum
intro: Rosa lutetiana subsp. dumetorum, also known as Rosa dumetorum, is a subspecies of wild rose that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It is a perennial shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, valued for its medicinal properties and various health benefits. The plant is traditionally used to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and its flowers, leaves, and roots are used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures. Rosa dumetorum is also used in folk medicine to treat conditions like arthritis, gout, and respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
